options.content:
# Teleop command path: FE → dma_bridge → supervisor → dma.ethercat

Each row in **"Full command-path traces"** is **one stitched trace** spanning every hop. Click the **Trace ID** to open the tree:

```
dma_bridge          bridge.taskspace      (FE / bridge origin)
  phantom_runtime   supervisor.taskspace  (IK)
  phantom_runtime   control_tick → write  (policy → /desired)
  dma-ethercat      dma.apply_command     (EtherCAT terminal hop)
```

**Only a *fresh-frame*, sampled (~1/s) command merges into one trace** (it is parented onto the bridge's frame span). Latched / idle `control_tick`s stay separate and carry a *link* back to the most-recent frame — that is by design, so do not be alarmed by lone `control_tick` roots when you run a raw `{ name="control_tick" }` search in Explore.

Datasource: **Tempo**. The panels below are just saved TraceQL — see `gaia/transport.md` for how this dashboard was built and how to extend it.

panels.title: Full command-path traces (FE → EtherCAT)   ·   { name="bridge.taskspace" } && { name="dma.apply_command" }
Supervisor merged with bridge   ·   { name="control_tick" } && { name="bridge.taskspace" }

options.content: Node graph of the **traced** command path, from the OTel collector's `servicegraph` connector. Edges = `client → server` from parent/child span pairs across services; only the traced request path appears (not the 500 Hz streaming queues). NOTE: the collector's Prometheus exporter prefixes metrics with `dma_debug_`, so Grafana's built-in Tempo *Service Graph* tab (which expects the unprefixed name) stays empty — these panels query the prefixed `dma_debug_traces_service_graph_request_total` directly. Empty until command-path traces flow (run the supervisor + move a Quest frame).

panels.title: Service-graph edges (request counts) — reliable companion to the node graph
